title: Lush Egyptian Lentils Soup
description: A floral, creamy and festive Egyptian lentil soup infused with dried mint and served in a copper mug, perfect for a sattvic-friendly meal.
instructions: Wash the lentils thoroughly and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the cumin, coriander, and turmeric powders, stirring to coat the aromatics.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the lentils begin to soften.
Stir in the dried mint and coconut milk, then season with salt to taste.
Continue simmering uncovered for 10 minutes until the soup thickens and the lentils are tender.
Finish by adding fresh parsley and a splash of lemon juice, then ladle into a copper mug and serve warm.
